meta.title: Survival after status epilepticus — 5-year mortality by stratum (Alanís-Bernal)
meta.authors: Alanís-Bernal M, Campos-Fernández D, Abraira L, et al., Santamarina E
meta.outcome: Cumulative mortality over 5 years after non-anoxic status epilepticus, by ACD score band and by Lattanzi aetiological category.
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. Cumulative mortality over 5 years after an episode of non-anoxic status epilepticus (SE), from Alanís-Bernal et al. (Epilepsia 2026, n=786). Pick a stratum to see how mortality accrues over time.

Strata. The whole cohort; by ACD score band (≤6 / 7–9 / ≥10); and by Lattanzi aetiology of acute symptomatic SE (triggering factors / acute toxic / acute-secondary CNS / acute-primary CNS).

Provenance of the numbers. The 5-year endpoints for the Lattanzi groups (20.0% / 18.4% / 58.3% / 61.3%) and the overall cohort (50.3%) are the paper's exact reported values; the curve shapes and the per-ACD-band figures are digitized from the published Kaplan–Meier figures (Figs 1–3) and are approximate. Mortality after SE is front-loaded — nearly half of all deaths occur within the first 2 years.

Scope. Adults / adolescents (≥16 years), non-anoxic SE. Population-level estimates — always apply clinical judgement.

model.selector_info: Choose the cohort or subgroup to view its cumulative-mortality curve over 5 years. Lattanzi 5-year endpoints are exact (paper text); ACD-band curves and shapes are digitized from the Kaplan–Meier figures.

Overall (all patients).info: All 786 patients with non-anoxic status epilepticus. 5-year mortality 50.3% (nearly half of deaths within the first 2 years).

ACD ≤6.info: Low ACD score (0–6). ~20% 5-year mortality. Digitized from the Kaplan–Meier curve.

ACD 7-9.info: Intermediate ACD score (7–9). ~38% 5-year mortality. Digitized from the Kaplan–Meier curve.

ACD ≥10.info: High ACD score (≥10). Survival falls below 50% by 2 years; ~77% 5-year mortality. Digitized from the Kaplan–Meier curve.

Triggering factors.info: Acute symptomatic SE from triggering factors in epilepsy (Lattanzi). 5-year mortality 20.0% (exact, from the paper text).

Acute toxic.info: Acute symptomatic SE, drug/alcohol intoxication or withdrawal (Lattanzi). 5-year mortality 18.4% (exact).

model.note: Cumulative mortality after non-anoxic status epilepticus. Lattanzi and overall 5-year endpoints are the paper's exact figures; per-ACD-band values and curve shapes are digitized from the Kaplan–Meier plots (approximate). Always apply clinical judgement.

provenance.source: Alanís-Bernal et al., Epilepsia 2026 (doi 10.1002/epi.70363, PMID 42396999). Curve shapes digitized (WebPlotDigitizer approach, paper_engine) from Figure 1 (overall), Figure 2 (Lattanzi aetiology), Figure 3A (ACD bands). Lattanzi 5-year mortality endpoints (primary CNS 61.3%, secondary CNS 58.3%, acute toxic 18.4%, triggering factors 20.0%) and overall (50.3%) anchored to the exact values reported in the Results text; ACD-band per-time values are figure-read.
provenance.notes: Cumulative-mortality curves, 61 monthly points (0-60 mo) per stratum, monotone non-decreasing. Review overlays: 2_digitize/out/alanis/{acd_km,lattanzi,overall}_review_overlay.png. ACD-band y-calibration refit on plateau anchors (≤6→0.80, ≥10→0.23 survival) so ACD≥10 crosses <50% survival at 2yr per the paper. curve_metric:mortality suppresses the COSY/driving panel.
